2604.08964.title: Breaking Block Boundaries: Anchor-based History-stable Decoding for Diffusion Large Language Models
2604.08964.paper_summary: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) have recently become a promising alternative to autoregressive large language models (ARMs). Semi-autoregressive (Semi-AR) decoding is widely employed in base dLLMs and advanced decoding strategies due to its superior performance. However, our observations reveal that Semi-AR decoding suffers from inherent block constraints, which cause the decoding of many cross-block stable tokens to be unnecessarily delayed. To address this challenge, we systematically investigate the identification of stable tokens and present three key findings: (1) naive lookahead decoding is unreliable, (2) token stability closely correlates with convergence trend, and (3) historical information is isolated. Building on these insights, we propose Anchor-based History-stable Decoding (AHD), a training-free, plug-and-play dynamic decoding strategy. Specifically, AHD monitors the stability trend of tokens in real time through dynamic anchors. Once a token reaches stability, it initiates early cross-block decoding to enhance efficiency and performance. Extensive experiments across language, vision-language, and audio-language domains demonstrate that AHD simultaneously improves both performance and inference efficiency. Notably, AHD effectively reverses the performance degradation typically observed in existing advanced decoding acceleration strategies. For instance, on the BBH benchmark, our approach reduces decoding steps by 80% while improving performance by 3.67%.
2604.08964.paper_summary_zh: 擴散大型語言模型（dLLMs）最近成為自回歸大型語言模型（ARMs）的有前景的替代方案。半自回歸（Semi-AR）解碼因其卓越的性能而廣泛應用於基礎 dLLMs 和先進的解碼策略。然而，我們的觀察顯示，Semi-AR 解碼受到固有的區塊限制，導致許多跨區塊穩定標記的解碼不必要地延遲。為了解決這一挑戰，我們系統性地研究了穩定標記的識別，並提出三個關鍵發現：（1）天真的前瞻解碼不可靠，（2）標記穩定性與收斂趨勢密切相關，以及（3）歷史信息是孤立的。在這些見解的基礎上，我們提出了基於錨點的歷史穩定解碼（AHD），這是一種無需訓練的即插即用動態解碼策略。具體而言，AHD 通過動態錨點實時監控標記的穩定性趨勢。一旦標記達到穩定性，它便啟動早期跨區塊解碼，以提高效率和性能。在語言、視覺語言和音頻語言領域的廣泛實驗表明，AHD 同時提高了性能和推理效率。值得注意的是，AHD 有效地逆轉了在現有先進解碼加速策略中通常觀察到的性能下降。例如，在 BBH 基準測試中，我們的方法將解碼步驟減少了 80%，同時提高了 3.67% 的性能。
2604.08964.author: Shun Zou et.al.
2604.08964.authors: Shun Zou, Yong Wang, Zehui Chen, Lin Chen, Chongyang Tao, Feng Zhao, Xiangxiang Chu
